# AppQR

AppQR is a free, open source CLI tool and npm package that generates smart QR codes for mobile apps. A single QR code detects the user's device and redirects iOS users to the App Store and Android users to Google Play.

## What it does

- Generates a `redirect.html` file the developer uploads once to their own website
- Generates QR code images (PNG) that point to that hosted redirect file
- The redirect file decodes campaign URLs from the QR code itself and redirects based on device
- Supports separate campaign tracking parameters for Apple App Store and Google Play
- Works forever — no subscriptions, no third-party servers in the redirect chain

## Two modes

### Setup (run once per app)
Generates both `redirect.html` and the first `qr.png`. Developer uploads `redirect.html` to their website once and never touches it again.

### Campaign (run per campaign)
Generates only a new `qr.png` pointing to the already-hosted `redirect.html` with new campaign parameters encoded in the URL. No file upload needed.

## How to use as an npm package

```javascript
import { setup, campaign } from 'appqr'

// First time — generates redirect.html + qr.png
const result = await setup({
  ios: 'https://apps.apple.com/app/id123456789',
  android: 'https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.myapp',
  hostedUrl: 'https://myapp.com/go',
  iosParams: 'ct=campaign_name&pt=12345&mt=8',
  androidParams: 'utm_source=instagram&utm_campaign=launch&utm_medium=paid',
  outputPath: './public/go'
})
// result.redirectPath — upload this file to hostedUrl on the user's site
// result.qrPath       — the QR code image to use in marketing

// Per campaign — generates qr.png only, no upload needed
const qr = await campaign({
  ios: 'https://apps.apple.com/app/id123456789',
  android: 'https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.myapp',
  hostedUrl: 'https://myapp.com/go',
  iosParams: 'ct=instagram_launch&pt=12345&mt=8',
  androidParams: 'utm_source=instagram&utm_campaign=launch&utm_medium=paid',
  outputPath: './qrs/instagram'
})
// qr.qrPath — use this QR image for the campaign
```

## How to use as a CLI

```bash
# Install
npm install -g appqr

# First time setup
appqr setup \
  --ios "https://apps.apple.com/app/id123456789" \
  --android "https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.myapp" \
  --url "https://myapp.com/go" \
  --ios-params "ct=launch&pt=12345&mt=8" \
  --android-params "utm_source=launch&utm_campaign=v1"

# New campaign QR
appqr campaign \
  --ios "https://apps.apple.com/app/id123456789" \
  --android "https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.myapp" \
  --url "https://myapp.com/go" \
  --ios-params "ct=instagram_launch&pt=12345&mt=8" \
  --android-params "utm_source=instagram&utm_campaign=launch&utm_medium=paid" \
  --output ./qrs/instagram
```

## URL validation rules

- iOS URL must start with: `https://apps.apple.com`
- Android URL must start with: `https://play.google.com/store`
- hostedUrl must be a full URL starting with `https://`

## Apple App Store campaign parameters

| Parameter | Description | Example |
|-----------|-------------|---------|
| ct | Campaign token — your label | ct=instagram_launch |
| pt | Provider token — numeric Apple ID | pt=12345 |
| mt | Media type — always 8 for apps | mt=8 |

## Google Play campaign parameters (standard UTM)

| Parameter | Description | Example |
|-----------|-------------|---------|
| utm_source | Traffic source | utm_source=instagram |
| utm_medium | Marketing medium | utm_medium=paid |
| utm_campaign | Campaign name | utm_campaign=launch |
| utm_content | Specific ad or creative (optional) | utm_content=banner_v1 |
| utm_term | Keyword for search campaigns (optional) | utm_term=todo+app |

## Architecture notes for agents

- The `redirect.html` is a generic static file — it contains zero campaign data
- All campaign data is encoded as base64url in the QR code URL itself (?d=...)
- The redirect file decodes this client-side in the browser using atob()
- No AppQR servers are involved after setup — the redirect runs entirely on the user's own website
- Telemetry: one anonymous counter ping per run, no personal data. Opt out with APPQR_NO_TELEMETRY=1

## Common agent workflows

### Scaffolding a new app marketing site
1. Call `setup()` with the app's store URLs and the URL where redirect.html will live
2. Copy `result.redirectPath` to the correct location in the site's public directory
3. Use `result.qrPath` as the QR code image on the landing page

### Generating a campaign QR for a marketing push
1. Call `campaign()` with the base store URLs, campaign params, and output path
2. Use `qr.qrPath` as the QR image for that campaign's materials

### Checking if a URL is valid before calling
```javascript
import { validateIosUrl, validateAndroidUrl } from 'appqr'
validateIosUrl('https://apps.apple.com/app/id123')    // true
validateAndroidUrl('https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.app') // true
validateIosUrl('https://example.com')                  // false
```

## Privacy

AppQR sends one anonymous counter ping per run. No URLs, no campaign data, no personal information, no device identifiers are ever collected or transmitted. Set APPQR_NO_TELEMETRY=1 to opt out entirely.

## Source

https://github.com/yourusername/appqr

## MCP server (Model Context Protocol)

AppQR ships with a built-in MCP server. AI agents that support MCP can call AppQR natively without CLI knowledge.

### Start the MCP server

```bash
appqr mcp
```

### Add to agent config (Claude Desktop, Cursor, etc.)

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "appqr": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["appqr", "mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

### MCP tools available

**appqr_setup** — First time setup. Generates redirect.html and qr.png.
Parameters: ios (required), android (required), hostedUrl (required), iosParams (optional), androidParams (optional), outputPath (optional, default ./appqr-output)

**appqr_campaign** — Generate a new campaign QR. redirect.html already on site. Generates qr.png only.
Parameters: ios (required), android (required), hostedUrl (required), iosParams (optional), androidParams (optional), outputPath (optional, default ./appqr-campaign)

**appqr_validate** — Validate store URLs before generating.
Parameters: ios (optional), android (optional)

### Typical agent workflow for scaffolding a marketing site

1. Agent calls appqr_setup with the app's store URLs and the URL where redirect.html will live on the site
2. Agent copies result.redirectPath to the correct public directory of the site
3. Agent uses result.qrPath as the QR image on the landing page
4. For each future campaign, agent calls appqr_campaign with campaign-specific params
